      The dramatic triangle — re-named the Low Drama Triangle by Possibility Management — is in fact an irresponsible expression of the 4 feelings:

      Irresponsible anger → Persecutor

      Irresponsible sadness → Victim


      Irresponsible fear → Rescuer


      Irresponsible joy → Gremlin (the part of us that wants to see the circus catch fire and that, in truth, enjoys Low Drama)

      Adding this discovery to the one that feelings are valuable sources of information and energy — and can be used responsibly to create what you want — the High Drama Triangle was created. Where the same anger, sadness, fear, and joy, previously used to create Low Drama irresponsibly, can be used responsibly to create. And by create I mean: make agreements, negotiate, make decisions, choose, set limits, communicate, and the like.

      Responsible anger → The Guardian

      Responsible sadness → The Communicator


      Responsible fear → The Designer / Magician


      Responsible joy → The Space-Holder

      The invitation then is to be an alchemist of feelings whenever children are creating Low Drama. You take each one of them, listen to them, and help them navigate their feelings from irresponsible to responsible — identifying the information behind the feeling and the invitation that the bubbling energy makes — not suppressing them, but truly listening to what is behind them — and in this way creating in collaboration.

      Do this a few times and you will be surprised by how quickly children mature emotionally.

      Gameworlds — Growing outside the thought prisons.

      You have spent your entire life swimming in Gameworlds and never noticed the water.

      A Gameworld is a field of commitment to an agreed-upon purpose, context, codex, and rules of engagement.

      Every human interaction you have ever had happened within a Gameworld. Every single one, without exception.

      You wake up in the morning. You make coffee. You sit in a meeting. You argue with someone you love. You pay for lunch. You go to sleep. Each of these is a Gameworld, or multiple Gameworlds nested inside one another.

      Think for example of the Post Office. To play in the Post Office Gameworld, you take your package to the Post Office building during business hours. You stand silently in a single queue. A minimum of twenty minutes. When you reach the front, a public employee may or may not decide to help you. You fill out a form by hand. You pay what they ask. And "neither snow, nor rain, nor sun, nor the darkness of night" prevents the deliverers from delivering your correspondence.

      The purpose of the Post Office Gameworld is to generate profit. How do you know? Because if the Post Office delivers your correspondence on time but does not generate profit, the government closes it or privatizes it.

      Notice: you entered, and within seconds began to behave as if the Post Office's rules were natural laws. As if there were no other possible way to send a package. As if the queue, the form, the pen, the counter — as if all of it had always existed, in the same way that gravity has always existed.

      But someone invented it. Someone — one day, at a moment in history — created the Post Office's rules. Wrote them down. Enforced them. And now you stand in line for twenty minutes without questioning it.

      Human beings create a Gameworld, enter into its rules of engagement, and within seconds begin to behave as if those rules were true. Even if a moment before the rules were created from nothing.

      If you do not know that a Gameworld is a Gameworld, you think it is reality. (It is not.)

      If you think a Gameworld is real, you behave as if you were imprisoned in the Gameworlds of your life. (You are not.)

      Is Money a Gameworld? Yes. Is a family a Gameworld? Yes. A marathon race? A supermarket? A film production team? A blues band? A permaculture farm? Yes. Banks, multinationals, armies, churches, public schools, governments, NGOs, the job market, police forces, and Starbucks franchises? Yes.

      Each Gameworld is unique, but each Gameworld is built from the same components: its context, its rules of engagement, its codex, and its semi-permeable membrane that distinguishes who is inside and who is outside.

      Each Gameworld is as arbitrary and as little concrete reality as a pair of children playing make-believe in the backyard.

      All Gameworlds are temporary.

      Realizing that the institutions of civilization are mere Gameworlds is an awakening — an invitation to recognize that you had been handing over your center and your authority to false assumptions, to habitual thoughts, to imaginary constructions.

      The New Education — or Archan Education, from the culture of Archiarchy — is not being designed to make you believe in Gameworlds like the "Job Market," the "Economy," and "Academic Credentials," but rather to invite you to relate to real and important things such as clean drinking water, the environment, human well-being, and love for oneself and for others.

      Do not live imprisoned in Gameworlds as if they were the truth. They are not. Clean drinking water is real. Money, bitcoin, and the stock market are Gameworlds.

      Today, for the majority of the world's population — who believe in Gameworlds — it is easier to imagine the end of the world than the end of capitalism. That is not the future we want. That is why recognizing Gameworlds as Gameworlds from childhood is something so valuable.

      Gameworlds are extraordinary — they show the creativity and organizational capacity of the human animal. The problem is not playing in Gameworlds. The problem is forgetting that they are Gameworlds. Because when you forget, you begin to destroy real things to protect imaginary ones. And that has happened before. It is happening now.

      Ecocidal and tyrannical Gameworlds only continue to exist because people treat them as undeniable reality — instead of recognizing them for what they are: a Gameworld that hired a private armed force to implement its rules of engagement. The moment you recognize that, the power it has over you changes completely.

      ◈ LEARNING How learning actually happens — ADVANCED

      Necessity — The foundational ingredient of learning.

      "You cannot deliver a woman who is not pregnant." There is no point in giving answers to questions the child does not yet carry. The most efficient learning is that which sprouts from a real question or need. There are a series of things that are better taught only when the moment arrives and necessity knocks at the door: new languages, advanced technical skills, the history of unknown places irrelevant to the child's life, interesting but practically useless knowledge for their day-to-day…

      However, there are questions that are valuable to sow — particularly when it comes to the building of virtues and character. It is not because the question must sprout from the child that you cannot have been the one who planted the seed.

      A seed is not a sermon. It is a question that creates curiosity without demanding an immediate answer.

      What happens to trust when I tell a lie?

      And what happens when trust is gone?

      Questions like these work from within — and when the child lives a situation that activates them, the answer will be their own.

      The same principle applies to any discipline. Before speaking of Alexander the Great, discuss whether it would be possible for a young person in their early twenties to conquer dozens of countries.

      Before financial literacy, ask why people who earn the same salary live such different lives.

      Before Gandhi, question whether it is possible to win a war without violence.

      Before the water cycle, ask why the world's water has not run out after millions of years and billions of creatures drinking from it.

      Let the question come before the answer. It is a more natural, more enjoyable path — and far more efficient. You create curiosity. And curiosity switches on the learning sensors in the child's mind.

      Knowing how to work with real necessity and to plant a question before giving answers is one of the most valuable skills of a talented educator.

      Disciplines — Disciplines are means for the child's potential, not ends for technical knowledge.

      As seen in the article "When Virtue Ceased to Be the Heart of Education," the disciplines were originally designed to be means for the construction of virtues in the human being — and not ends of technical development.

      Mathematics, geometry, arts, poetry, philosophy, history, physical education, and many others are doors for the individual to explore their capacities, develop different parts of their brain, and build their character. Not a memorization of facts and information.

      A teacher who teaches geometry by asking "what is the minimum area a person needs to live with dignity?" is not teaching only geometry. They are using geometry to develop empathy, a sense of justice, and critical thinking. Geometry was the means. Virtue was the end.

      A physical education class where the child learns to lose without drama, to win without arrogance, and to keep trying even when they want to give up is not teaching sport. It is using sport to build willpower, humility, and resilience. Sport was the means. Character was the end.

      A teacher who teaches the history of Gandhi is not teaching dates and events. They are using the life of a real human being to show what courage, non-violence, and commitment to an ideal look like in practice. They can even go further — to question and investigate with the students what leads a human being to commit to something greater than themselves. What leads millions of people to join a movement? How does one take the lead on great changes when the system forces another path? Is there something he could have done and did not? Is there something he tried that did not work? With questions like these, the teacher feeds and nurtures the creative power of the children.

      So when delivering a discipline, carry the question, the purpose, the intention, and the action: how is this a bridge for the building of virtues and the development of the child?

      The time has come to stop passing content on autopilot without questioning. Our brilliance deserves to be used to design classes that cultivate the virtuous potential of the child — rather than merely guaranteeing a good grade on mass standardized exams. Life on this planet and the potential of the human being are only just beginning.

    • Choosing Your Value System.

      Every human being has a value system. Your value system is the things you value — the ruler you use to measure whether you are "doing well" in life or not. Because this internal ruler is so important, our life ends up being designed around it: our personality, our objectives, our choices, everything.

      This value system may have been consciously chosen by you — or unconsciously, through imitation of your surroundings and your upbringing within a specific value system.

      Traditional education teaches you how to win at the "game of life" — how to prepare yourself for society and the job market. It teaches you to play, work, and try to win in a pre-designed game.

      The Archan Education empowers you to design your own game. To choose your own value system. To create your own rules. Of course, virtues, responsibility, and wisdom will always be proposed as part of your game and your value system — and the example of how extraordinary and enjoyable life can be in this way will always be portrayed in the educator's own life. But the choice is yours. The adventure is yours.

      It is worth, then, to deepen this dialogue by looking closely at what these value systems actually are.

      In some spiritual communities the value system is to resemble the "master," to be peaceful, and to appear perfect.

      Among certain groups of friends it is to be the one who gets with the most guys or women.

      In the job market, whoever earns the most money and has the most power.

      In the church, whoever holds the highest position.

      And so on — each Gameworld carrying its own value system. And these value systems shaping all who adhere to them.

      There are people — and I invite you to reflect on whether you are one of them — who spend their entire lives running from one side to the other to serve value systems that, in truth, if you stop and reflect very carefully, are not even that important to them.

      This reminds me of that story of the businessman who finds an indigenous man lying and relaxing on the sand of the beach and says:

      — Hey, why don't you go to work? — asks the businessman.— What for? — the indigenous man replies.
      — To have money.
      — What for?
      — To buy things.
      — What for? ………………

      Fifteen minutes later, the businessman finally concludes, triumphant:

      — So that one day you can retire and relax on the beach doing nothing!

      The indigenous man looks at him:

      — But I am already doing that, you pineapple head.

      How much money do people spend today to take care of their mental health, their physical health, their emptiness and their anxiety — on purchases to fill that void, on trips to get a sip of dopamine. And all that spending, all that life, because they work so hard to get the money to pay for all the things that the pursuit of money caused.

      Do you really want your value system to be being attractive? Having a body X or Y? Having money? Looking like someone else? Having power over others? Is that the most you think life can be?

      Is that the kind of value system you want children to be trapped in?

      What if you designed your own value system? What if you decided what is valuable to you? Not what is valuable to your family. Not what capitalism would like to be valuable to you. Nor the beautiful phrase your internet coach says, like "schedule freedom" and the like. Perhaps you do not even want to have a schedule.

      It is YOUR life. What do you want the value system of YOUR life to be?

      What is valuable to me is clean drinking water, adventure, possibility, community, abundance of delicious food, children alive and flourishing, not treating time as a resource, a relaxed nervous system, 5 Bodies balanced and nourished, a state of presence, creating things for the world, generosity, dancing, being on the team of the people I love, being elegant, deep and alive conversations, learning new things, seeing Men thrive, seeing Woman thrive, seeing children Thrive — and so much more.

      That is what is valuable to me. And what is valuable to YOU?

      Be careful — it is very possible that your first answer will not be authentic. There is a high risk that your identity is entangled with the culture where you were born and the Gameworlds in which you play. Dare to venture beyond the obvious.

      When you begin to have clarity about your own values, you can build this with the children too — helping them to discover what is genuinely valuable to them. Not what others value. What they value.

      Have you thought about how beautiful that is? To grow up valuing what your heart truly cares about — instead of growing up chasing a life that was sold to you, and not created by you.

    • I'm with the Children — Now What?

      A practical guide from the Archan Education for mothers, fathers, and educators

      You are with a child. It may be your son or daughter, your student, your nephew or niece. And you ask yourself: what do I do now?

      This article is a practical answer to that question. It is not a theory — it is a structure you can apply today, at dinner, in the classroom, at the park. But before getting to the "how," we need to talk about the "who."

      Before anything: preparing the ground

      Being Worthy of Imitation

      Before working with children we need to work on ourselves. Being a virtuous, wise, alive, free, loving, generous, responsible, centered Human Being — all of this is an intrinsic part of being an educator, since every one of our interventions will pass through who we are, and will carry our psychic DNA (or Thoughtware) with it. So step 1: be worthy of imitation.

      Building the Bond

      Building the bond of connection with the child or children is the second step. Educating without this (emotional, psychic) connection is a form of abuse. The process of education is much lighter and much more of a "partnership" when this bond/connection is built.

      How to build the bond? By listening to the child, taking them into consideration, treating them with dignity, being on their team for the things that are very important to them, setting limits when there is risk to their integrity, respecting their limits and personal space, tending to their needs, supporting them in difficulties, getting to know them and letting them get to know you, keeping the harmless secrets, negotiating agreements, and being a safe space.

      Becoming a Hero or Heroine

      Children tend to imitate those they admire. If you are already doing well with the first mission of being worthy of imitation, and are doing well with building the bond of connection between you and the child, the next step is to become a Hero or Heroine to the child.

      For some children, being a symbol of freedom, adventure, overcoming, strength, safety, and possibility is what will make them see you as a Hero/Heroine; for others, being a symbol of beauty, enchantment, generosity, abundance, clarity, and inner peace is what will make you their Hero/Heroine. You can train to be a symbol of all of these qualities and fully meet the different needs of the children, distinguishing when it is the moment for one posture and when it is the moment for another.

      Another factor that counts greatly is how well the child perceives that you survive in the world. Do the other adults respect you? Do you belong? Can you take care of yourself? These observations give them a sense of whether it is safe or not to imitate you.

      And at the same time that they see all those virtues and that "safety" in you, they also need to have the minimal impression that you are of the same species — to see your human side.

      They will only imitate someone with whom they feel similar — whether in the present moment, or when you tell stories about when you were a child. This gives the impression that, even though you are different, in potential you are the same.

      And that is something you can reinforce because it is in fact true. We all as Human Beings have the potential to be like any other Human Being we admire.

      The practice: from encounter to closing

      With the ground prepared, the practice begins.

      1. CHECK-IN — FINDING THE X ON THE MAP

      Beginning now with the actual scenario, and moving past the preparation of the ground (which in the end is always happening…): the child arrives to be with you, or wakes up, or returns from school, or arrives at school.

      Our first recommendation is that you discover the X on each other's map. You share how you are arriving — a little of the universe within you. The child shares a little of theirs, and in this way you both know who is arriving and from where, with what questions, what impulses, what feelings and what emotions.

      2. LISTENING, OBSERVING, READING

      Before beginning action and intervention, observation is essential — a scanning of the child across the 5 Bodies.

      How are they arriving? — including and beyond what they say.

      What impulses do they have that they may not yet have words or distinctions to express?
      What urges are present?

      After this listening and observation — which does not need to be very long, but deserves its dedicated time of presence — you can begin.

      3. THE INTERVENING

      At the moment of intervening, the possibilities are infinite and unlimited. Even so, I bring some of those possibilities here to help myself and the reader with the applicability of the Archan Education; however, I remind you that it is not limited to these interventions.

      3.a — Playing their games with your context

      Children have games they already love and that delight them greatly, and those same "ordinary" games can provide great evolution and development if you bring your context (virtuous, generous, beautiful, and of Radical Responsibility) into the game. Acting from that context, making decisions from that context, mediating conflicts from that context — all of this opens a door to discovery and learning even in the midst of "ordinary" games. Be the Hero/Heroine while playing. Be Worthy of Imitation.

      3.b — Instigating curiosity and setting out on quest-ions

      Sometimes the child's soul is ready for a quest (adventure/journey) — a journey of discovery and curiosity. This particular energy can arise "out of nowhere" when something catches the child's attention. It can happen when you provide a distinct experience, or a very good question that begins the ignition, or when a difficulty or challenge invites the child to grow. These quests are quest-ions — questions that guide the child to a discovery.

      Discoveries are the true form of learning.

      As a Space-Holder you can train to instigate quest-ions, which is an extraordinarily valuable skill — or cultivate and feed a quest-ion whenever one arises spontaneously from the space. Or even reveal one hidden behind a challenge. Your work is to help the child formulate a good question (quest-ion), and that is all. Let the child self-direct the rest, and support them in what they ask for and need. Do not kill questions with answers.

      What would be a "good question" (quest-ion)?

      It is a question that creates possibilities instead of one that limits. For example:

      Ordinary quest-ions:

      How do I get rid of my anger?
      How do I earn money?
      How do I belong?

      Possibility quest-ions:

      How do I use my anger and why does it exist?
      How do I generate value and create abundance?
      How do I become the source of my own belonging?
      What is a bee?
      What is Time?
      How do I learn quickly anything I want?
      Why is my classmate so mean?
      How do I do a backflip?
      What does it take to be a Pirate in real life?

      Help the child refine the question. The question completely determines the journey.

      3.c — Designing games and adventures

      You do not need to — and should not — only play the games children already know. You can invent new games from nothing to:

      — have fun together,

      — practice a skill,
      — run an experiment that creates learning,
      — develop the body,
      — refine socio-emotional skills,
      — work on teamwork,
      — nourish a part of the brain, of thinking, or of perception,
      — help something or someone.

      Made on demand and by design, adapted to the children in front of you. And if you create one that is very interesting and enjoyable, you can share it with your educator friends — and the children will likely pass it along to other children.

      3.d — Sustaining practices and training

      Sometimes you come across the magical situation where the child really wants to learn something. They are already in the middle of their quest-ion and want to refine their skills in order to reach their objective. In that case you can be a trainer and support the child in their practice and training. If it is what the child wants, pour yourself into it: give feedback, correct, guide, try again and again — repeatedly, diversely, and enjoyably — until this new skill strengthens and becomes natural. Training and practicing to learn what you want is something truly enjoyable and enlivening. Help the child maintain their connection to the origin and the why — to the ion of the quest — when crossing the more challenging limits.

      3.e — Using Archan games

      We on the Archan Education research team also have a list of games we have distilled and collected — you can try them out! They are games that create opportunity for reflection, fun, learning, discovery, and possibilities.

      But we do not share this list so that you stop creating and discovering your own games and only use ours — I would not be capable of committing that crime against Humanity. Keep creating and inventing your own list! And share it with us and with the world. You are also a researcher of the Archan Education!

      Access the list at archaneeducation.com/experimentos.

      3.f — Using Tales

      Tales have a fascinating power over children — when well told, when interesting, they can open great doors of learning and discovery, growth and evolution. Use stories, create stories, and especially dialogue with the children about the tales; this helps to digest and extract all of their treasures.

      Through this digestion via dialogue, certain films can become high-quality pedagogical material, stories of figures from the past become a mirror of the human psyche, and the child traverses entire quest-ions through imagination and emotional and intellectual connection with every piece of the story.

      It is no coincidence that great teachers of humanity such as Jesus, Siddhartha, Plato, and many more always used stories, parables, myths, and anecdotes to share their knowledge.

      4. CHECK-OUT — CLOSING THE SPACE

      To close the experience, the day, or the moment, the check-out is a good way to extract, remember, digest, and absorb the discoveries made.

      Questions such as:

      what did you discover?
      what questions are you leaving with?
      what do you not want to forget?
      what do you want to improve on?
      what are you grateful for about today?

      These are questions that keep the learning and discovery happening even after the space has ended.

      One last important thing

      It is worth pointing out that this is a practical structure — condensed and focused on actions. For these actions to be truly effective, a significant amount of evolution, growth, and knowledge needs to happen on our side as educators.

      This is where the entire "theoretical" — yet genuinely important — part of the Archan Education comes in. For it is in this theory that we refine our premises about the world (Thoughtware) and the knowledge at our disposal to be more efficient in our actions.

      Simply following the formula of this article without feeding yourself on the pedagogical vision of Archan Education is like, in a medical surgery, using a scalpel on a living body — where the incision site is drawn — but without the technical knowledge of the tool, the execution, how deep it can go, how steady the hand needs to be, and so on… Following a dotted line with a scalpel without being an experienced physician brings high risks to the surgery. Not because the dotted line was wrong, nor because there was something wrong with the scalpel, but because the person operating lacked the matrix of experience and knowledge.

      That is why we strongly recommend reading the section: [archaneeducation.com/filosofia1] where you can get to know and deepen your understanding of the pedagogical and philosophical vision of the Archan Education. The knowledge there is a gateway to a journey of personal transformation and evolution so that you become a Wise, Virtuous, and Skilled Educator.

    • Thoughtful Experiments

      An Extraordinary Educator, Mother, Father is a Free and Natural Adult who has healed a great quantity of the baggage of their past (most likely through Emotional Healing Processes) and has gone through various Initiations into Adult Maturity — building enough Matrix in their 5 Bodies to Hold Space that is Safe and Fertile for human offspring. They are also someone who has studied, experimented, and researched the nature of the child and of the Human Being. Someone who lives in constant High Drama, who has connected with their Archetypal Resources & has an intimate relationship with Gaia. Someone who has developed their Multiple Intelligences & knows how to play, curiosity-navigate, explore, and have High Level Fun like no one else. Someone at peace with life, at peace with the present, with a Small Now, Centered, with a Personal Space Bubble, Sword of Clarity, and Gremlin on a leash — working side by side to navigate spaces that are non-linear, non-hierarchical, and extraordinary. Someone with the Power of Stories in their hands, and gifted by time and detached observation with a sobriety about life. And finally, someone endowed with Generosity, Lovingness, and Kindness — whether naturally arising or self-constructed.

      All of us are on this journey. It is recommended to move at a good rhythm in order to enjoy the benefits of such achievements of personality — but without pressure or self-demand, for those bitter the soul more than they purify it. A solemn, rhythmic, enjoyable, and steady pace.

      The journey of each person to acquire all of these skills and qualities is certainly very unique and particular. But there are without doubt paths, possibilities, initiations, and environments that invite such skills to be developed in more efficient and consistent ways than others. We leave here then a list of initiations, paths, experiments, and studies that, when walked, will certainly bring you closer to all of these qualities and skills:

      READ THE WEBSITES LINKED ON THE TEXT ABOVE (AND DO THEIR EXPERIMENTS).

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.01

      In the text "How to Become an Extraordinary Educator / Mother / Father?" you will notice various words with links attached to them. Each link is a website that presents a new transformative concept to empower the Extraordinary Educator in you. Read all of those websites — they may contain many treasures for your journey.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.01 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Point.

      PARTICIPATE IN 4 EXPAND THE BOX TRAININGS AND 10 POSSIBILITY LABs.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.02

      These trainings are transformative immersions that will help you heal various emotional burdens mentioned in the texts — and will also be a place to learn and practice essential skills of an Extraordinary Educator.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.02 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 2 Matrix Points.

      READ ALL THE BOOKS IN THE ARCHAN EDUCATION CURATED LIBRARY.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.03

      In the Archan Education curated library you will find a series of carefully selected books with an extraordinarily high density of valuable information about holding safe and fertile spaces for children. Reading them will greatly empower your capacities as a Father / Mother / Educator.Access the books of the Archan Education curated library here.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.03 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      VISIT 5 RADICALLY ALTERNATIVE SCHOOLS.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.04

      Seeing in practice the work of these alternative schools — which live day-to-day a pedagogy aligned with Archan pedagogy — will bring you many reflections and Thoughtware transformations. Don't forget to bring your BeepBook to take notes.Check our list of alternative schools.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.04 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      GO THROUGH 100+ EMOTIONAL HEALING PROCESSES.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.05

      Emotional Healing Processes are the most efficient way to heal traumas, emotional wounds, and thought patterns — and to release baggage. This is an indispensable step in the journey of an Extraordinary Educator.

      Schedule your first Emotional Healing Process and begin your journey.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.05 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      WATCH ALL THE FILMS AND DOCUMENTARIES IN THE ARCHAN EDUCATION CURATED LIBRARY.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.06

      Archetypal films have the power to transform us, awaken us, and invoke our archetypal lineage to move through us and do its work on earth. Don't miss these transformative films — build matrix while enjoying the magic of cinema.

      Access the films and documentaries of the Archan Education curated library here.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.06 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      INTERVIEW 5 MOTHERS WHO APPLIED THE CONTINUUM CONCEPT WITH THEIR CHILDREN.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.07

      The Continuum Concept is simple and transformative. Start by reading the book (link available in our curated library) and then schedule 5 online interviews with mothers who applied the Continuum at home. This way you can learn more about the practical applications and also gather the testimonials needed to help you decide whether to apply it yourself.Check our list of Continuum mothers available for interviews. (under construction)

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.07 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      LEAVE YOUR TRADITIONAL JOB AND LIVE FROM YOUR NON-MATERIAL VALUE.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.08

      Working a traditional job, doing something you don't love, and serving someone who only wants to make money is a waste of life. Discover your Non-Material Value and live by creating good things for the world.

      In doing so, you become a living example for the child that they too have this possibility.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.08 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      WATCH ALL THE YOUTUBE VIDEOS IN THE ARCHAN EDUCATION CURATED LIBRARY.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.09

      We have selected a series of YouTube gems on extraordinary education. Watch all of these videos, take notes, and integrate your learnings. Explaining to someone what you learned in each video right after watching it will also help you integrate the knowledge.

      Access the YouTube videos of the Archan Education curated library here.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.09 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      SPEND A WEEK CAMPING WITH A CHILD.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.10

      Pure contact with nature, without devices, without work, without commitments. Cooking over a fire, listening to the sounds of the forest, telling stories, and simply existing together. This builds Matrix in Radical Relating and Space-Holding. Choose a somewhat isolated location, immersed in nature, but safe. Let friends know your location and maintain a rhythm of daily contact. Rent some tents and go camping with the children. Discover together how to spend the days — and make sure you bring no studying or work for those days. This is an initiation into creativity, non-linearity, connection, and aliveness. You may be surprised by how many opportunities you will have to practice your Space-Holding with the children.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.10 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      READ ALL THE BOOKS THAT ENTHUSE YOU FROM THE POSSIBILITY MANAGEMENT BOOK CURATION.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.11

      An enlivening list of books awaits you in the PM book curation. Access the list by clicking here.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.11 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      READ ALL THE ARTICLES IN THE ARCHAN EDUCATION CURATED LIBRARY.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.12

      Some readings truly move our internal structures. The articles in our curated library have that intention. There we have selected texts that can genuinely add to your journey of development as a child Permaculturist. Read all the articles to collect this treasure.Access the articles of the Archan Education curated library here.

      After completing this experiment, please register Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.12 in your free account at StartOver.xyz. This Experiment is worth 1 Matrix Points.

      ATTEND STORYTELLING COURSES.

      Matrix Code ARCHANEDUCATION.13

      Some readings truly move our internal structures. The articles in our curated library have that intention.     • StartOver.xyz

      NOTE: This website is a Bubble in the Bubble Map of the free-to-play, massively-multiplayer, online-and-offline, thoughtware-upgrade, matrix-building, personal-transformation, adventure-game called StartOver.xyz. It is a doorway to experiments that upgrade your thoughtware so you can create more possibility. Your knowledge is what you think about. Your thoughtware is what you use to think with. When you change your thoughtware, you go through a liquid state as your mind reorganizes itself. Liquid states can bring up transformational feelings and emotions. By upgrading your thoughtware you build matrix to hold more consciousness and leave behind a life of reactivity. No one can upgrade your thoughtware for you. No one can stop you from upgrading your thoughtware. Our theory is that when we collectively build 1,000,000 new Matrix Points we will change the morphogenetic field of the human race for the better. Please choose responsibly to read this website. Reading this whole website is worth 1 Matrix Point. Doing any of the experiments earns you additional Matrix Points. Please use Matrix Code THINKING.00 to log your Matrix Point for reading this website on StartOver.xyz. Thank you for playing full out!

      Cookie Use
      We use cookies to ensure a smooth browsing experience. By accepting, you agree the use of cookies. Learn More

      Settings
      Decline All
      ×
      What Are Cookies?

      Cookies are bits of information (memory files) stored in your browser by sites you visit. As is common practice with almost all websites, this site uses cookie to improve your experience by remembering your preferences and enable other cookie-based features (e.g. analytics).

      Our Cookies

      When you submit data through a form such as those found on contact pages or comment forms, cookies may be set to remember your user details for future correspondence.

      In order to provide you with a better experience and remember your preferences, we need to set cookies so that this information can be called whenever you interact with a page.

      Third-Party cookies

      In special cases, we also use cookies provided by trusted third parties like Google Analytics. Third party analytics are used to track and measure usage of this site so that we can continue to produce engaging content.

      How To Disable Cookies?

      Most browsers let you to refuse to accept cookies. See your browser Help or Tool for how to do this. Be aware that disabling cookies may affect the functionality of this and many other websites you visit. Therefore, it is recommended that you do not disable cookies.

      Cookie Use
      We use cookies to ensure a smooth browsing experience. By continuing we assume you accept the use of cookies.
